EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical Storm Isaias affected the area on July 29th and July 30th. Even though the center passed about 100 miles south of the southern coast of Puerto Rico, given that the tropical cyclone was large in size, the associated impacts extended well north and northeast. Widespread 5 to 10 inches of rain was reported in Puerto Rico along with 30 to 40 mph sustained winds with gusts as high as 60-70 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mudslide reported along Road PR-763 KM 0.7 in Barrio Borinquen.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tropical wave combined with an upper-level trough to result in enhanced thunderstorm activity across interior and western PR.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical Storm Laura affected the area on August 22nd. Sustained winds between 30-40 mph with higher gusts were common, however, some areas in southeast PR reported gust to near hurricane force. Rainfall accumulations for the most part generally ranged between 1 and 4 inches, resulting in areas of flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Fallen tree affected Road PR-853 KM 11.1 in Carolina. Road was impassable.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A man died due to flash flooding in an unnamed small creek in Sector Las Canelas between Arecibo y Hatillo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant low-level moisture in southerly flow resulted in areas of heavy showers across portions of the San Juan metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency manager reported Calle Luchetti, Calle Julian Blanco, Avenida Condado, and the Marginal Baldority were flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of an upper-level trough and moisture associated with a tropical wave produced moderate to heavy rains across portions of northeast, interior, and northwest PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported along Sector Seburuquillo due to a small stream overflowing its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper-level trough and sufficient moisture combined with local and diurnal effects to generate afternoon shower and thunderstorm, especially across the northwestern quadrant of Puerto Rico and the San Juan metro area. Across the San Juan metro area, a persistent streamer developed and resulted in rainfall accumulations in excess of 3-4 inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding reported along Roads 28 and 5 in the Urbanization of Villa Nevares behind Cosvi.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper-level trough and sufficient moisture combined with local and diurnal effects to generate afternoon shower and thunderstorm, especially across the northwestern quadrant of Puerto Rico and the San Juan metro area. Across the San Juan metro area, a persistent streamer developed and resulted in rainfall accumulations in excess of 3-4 inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding reported along Calle Manila.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A significant northerly swell from distant Hurricane Teddy, which tracked well north of the area, resulting in coastal flooding across some areas along the north coast of PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Coastal flooding reported along Road PR-187 near Pi��ones around Playa Vaciatalega in Loiza.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A significant northerly swell from distant Hurricane Teddy, which tracked well north of the area, resulting in coastal flooding across some areas along the north coast of PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three houses near the beach were affected by coastal flooding. These houses were located in Bajura sector, Barrio Membrillo of Camuy. Debris was also found inside the houses.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture combined with local and diurnal effects to result in the development of showers and isolated thunderstorms across northwestern Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported along Road PR-125 near Residencial Andres Mendez Liciaga.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical wave affected the area, with numerous shower and thunderstorm activity, especially across eastern PR. 2 to 4 inches of rain fell.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rio Guayanes went out of its banks, flooding road PR-3.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant low-level moisture in a southerly wind flow combined with local effects and diurnal heating to generate shower and thunderstorm activity across northern PR, including the San Juan metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong Winds related to a strong thunderstorm resulted in damage to a house in which part of the roof made of zinc was gone. In addition, a tree fell. This occurred along Road Camino Valentin, Barrio Buena Vista, Sector Nueve.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A 61 year old male was found from New Jersey was found dead in Black Eagle Beach in Rincon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 61 year old male was found from New Jersey was found dead in Black Eagle Beach in Rincon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A male tourist from NY drowned Cayo Tierra Beach in Vieques.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A male tourist from NY died on Cayo Tierra Beach in Vieques.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A couple of tourists swept away and drowned due to rip currents in the beaches of Loiza and Isabela.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three people were swept away by rip currents at Pi��ones Beach in Loiza. Two were able to swim back and carried the other to shore but the person had no vital signs. The person who died was a tourist from Orlando, Florida.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A 75-year-old man was reported missing after he went snorkeling in Playa Guayan��s. His body was later recovered.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 75-year-old man was reported missing after he went snorkeling in Playa Guayan��s. His body was later recovered.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved over the area. This cold front along with abundant low to mid-level moisture and a trough aloft resulted areas of heavy rainfall activity across the north-central and northeastern portions of Puerto Rico during this period.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road PR-962 between KM 0.6 and KM 0.8 was flooded with 8 feet of water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A couple of tourists swept away and drowned due to rip currents in the beaches of Loiza and Isabela.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tourist from Maryland swept away by rip currents into deep waters and drowned at Middles beach in Isabela.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A 47-year-old male drowned while attempting to rescue two people who were caught in a rip current.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 47-year-old male drowned while attempting to rescue two people who were caught in a rip current in Dorado.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two bodies (a male and a female) were rescued by the U.S. Coast Guard in La Pocita, Pi��ones area in Loiza.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two bodies (a male and a female) were rescued by the U.S. Coast Guard in La Pocita, Pi��ones area in Loiza County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Female swept by rip current and her partner tried to rescue her and both drowned at La Concha Beach in Condado. The couple were from Georgia.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Female swept by rip current and her partner tried to rescue her and both drowned at La Concha Beach in Condado. The couple were from Georgia.
